elections.note: Certified final 63,255 ballots is solid (SoS Voter Participation Statistics; matches county certified report, Clinton 39,199/63.87%). The county DocumentCenter for the Nov 8 2016 general archives only a Nov-30 'Final Unofficial Post-Election Night Report' and the certified results page -- no 8 PM first-night report exists in the archive. Wayback is blocked in this environment, so the election-night first-tranche count is not sourceable. Pre-adoption (e-pollbooks first used 2018).

First adoption year for e-pollbooks (post). County 'First Unofficial Election Night Report' = 21,732 voters cast (27.81% of 78,135 reg). The header 'Ballots Cast: 43,527' is ballot CARDS (~2 cards/voter); the voter/ballot count is 21,732 (Governor 'Times Cast 21,732'), consistent with the SoS certified total of 57,132 voters. Report internal clock reads 6:25 PM (generation time; published election night). Count barely moved overnight -- the 'Last Unofficial Election Night Report' (10:52 PM) was 21,774 voters (+42); the remaining ~62% were tallied in the post-election canvass. Pct = 21,732/57,132.

County 'First Unofficial Election Night Report' timestamped 8:00:59 PM = 'Cards Cast: 42,914' (188 of 191 precincts, 98.24%). Equals ballots/voters here -- the President contest drew ~42,300 votes (Biden 31,270 + Trump 10,392 + others), so essentially one card per voter. High election-night share reflects 2020 universal vote-by-mail (COVID): a very large early-VBM batch was already processed by 8 PM. Pct = 42,914/73,269.

County '1st Unofficial Election Night Report' timestamped 8:01 PM = 20,390 voters cast (24.43% of 83,471 reg), 100% Vote-by-Mail (Election Day 0), 198 of 200 precincts. 'Last Unofficial Election Night Report' was 10:46 PM. Pct = 20,390/50,788.

County 'First Unofficial Election Night Report' timestamped 8:01 PM = 22,504 voters cast (26.43% of 85,150 reg), all 204 precincts. 'Last Unofficial Election Night Report' (12:35 AM) = 26,160. Much lower election-night share than 2020 presidential (58.6%), which had the pandemic universal-mail surge; e-pollbooks (2018) did not raise the election-night share here. Pct = 22,504/66,634.
